🔬 Computational Chemistry Report

ORCA Quantum Chemical Calculations Analysis

Generated: March 14, 2026 | Molecules: 45 | Dataset: 105 calculations

  • 1. Executive Summary

📋 1. Executive Summary

This report presents comprehensive computational chemistry results for 45 molecular systems analyzed using ORCA quantum chemistry software. A total of 105 calculations were performed covering 3 electronic states (ground and excited states).

45
Molecules Studied
105
Total Calculations
3
Electronic States
67
IR Spectra

Key Findings

  • Energy Range: -887.3591 to -75.7455 Eh
  • Average HOMO-LUMO Gap: 5.14 ± 1.20 eV
  • Computational Methods: DFT/B3LYP, DFT/B3LYP with VWN functional 5 instead of RPA, DFT/WB97X, HF
  • Vibrational Data: 67 IR spectra, 30 Raman spectra available

⚗️ 2. Computational Methods

All calculations were performed using the ORCA quantum chemistry program package. The following computational methods and parameters were employed:

Parameter Value Description
Electronic Structure Method DFT/B3LYP Primary computational method
Number of Calculations 105 Total geometry optimizations and single points
Electronic States S0, S0P, VG Ground and excited state calculations
Vibrational Analysis Frequency calculations performed IR and Raman spectroscopy prediction

🔮 4. Electronic Structure

HOMO (Highest Occupied Molecular Orbital) and LUMO (Lowest Unoccupied Molecular Orbital) energy levels provide insight into the electronic properties, reactivity, and optical characteristics of the molecules.

Orbital Visualization Settings
10
🔮 Orbital Energy Diagram

Legend: Solid = Occupied, Dashed = Virtual. Bold lines = HOMO/LUMO.
